attacking_style: Pure sprinter relying on lead-out delivery rather than independent attacking. Requires organized launch at 800m-250m from the line on flat finishes.
team_tactics: Quick-Step lead-out train deployment from 3km out, with Mørkov as final pilot delivering at 800m. Depends entirely on domestique positioning and cannot recover from tactical errors.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finishes only. Exposed and dropped on even shallow climbs (4.8% average), eliminating him from contention in stage races with any vertical gain.
vulnerability: Concentration lapses at critical high-speed moments (roundabouts, positioning switches). Cannot sustain form across multiple consecutive days. Vulnerable to blocking tactics in final 250m.
key_patterns: Loses wheels or position at technical high-speed moments despite good initial placement (2 instances)
Fades in final 250m when lead-out disrupted, cannot finish independently (2 instances)
Dropped immediately on any sustained climbing, even shallow gradients eliminate him (evidence: 4.8% climb)
Shows form transfer ability across race types when fresh (Handzame to Catalunya stage 1)
form_trajectory: negative: positioned well but fading in sprints (5th at BinckBank), plus critical errors under pressure suggest declining sharpness
